dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI edged out the day on a very thin but net-positive signal: Codex was name-checked in a comment praising AI tools for making a weekend macOS app feasible, and there were no direct negative OpenAI comments to offset it [E1].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won because its only meaningful mention was favorable, with Codex grouped among tools that helped lower the barrier to shipping a useful app quickly [E1]. The coverage was sparse, so this was not a strong victory, but it was the cleanest positive signal among the tracked providers.

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex appeared as one example in a broader comment arguing that AI coding tools now let developers cross platform boundaries and ship small useful apps over a weekend [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude picked up one concrete boost from comments crediting LLM coding with making the slouch app feasible and treating Claude as a practical coding helper, but Claude Code was also singled out as a terminal UI 'web abomination,' which pulled the day back down [E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini was mentioned once as part of a broader 'great-ai-unlock' claim that modern AI coding tools make quick cross-platform prototyping much easier, but there was no deeper discussion beyond that [E4].

evidence.summary: In the Posturr thread, a top-level comment described a 'great-ai-unlock' and explicitly included Codex among tools that now let developers build cross-platform software quickly.

The Posturr discussion explicitly named Claude in favorable terms: commenters said LLM coding made the app possible and one experienced Mac developer offered help 'if Claude isn't sorting out' certain issues.
In the xdgctl thread, a top-level comment praised the app for not being another web stack inside the terminal and explicitly called out 'claude code' as the kind of terminal experience it disliked.
In the Posturr thread, Gemini was name-checked alongside other AI coding tools in a comment arguing that such systems now enable rapid weekend prototyping across unfamiliar platforms.
